seoTitle: Paynesville vs Peaceful Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Paynesville and Peaceful Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Paynesville vs Peaceful Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Paynesville and Peaceful Bay.
heading: Paynesville vs Peaceful Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paynesville, Western Australia and Peaceful Bay, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paynesville has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Peaceful Bay (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paynesville or Peaceful Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paynesville has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Peaceful Bay scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paynesville scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Peaceful Bay scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paynesville or Peaceful Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paynesville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
